title: Zephyranthes elwesii
taxonomy: Zephyranthes elwesii is a species of flowering plant in the family Amaryllidaceae. It belongs to the genus Zephyranthes, which includes over 70 species of plants commonly known as zephyr lilies or rain lilies. The genus Zephyranthes is further classified under the subfamily Amaryllidoideae, a group of flowering plants known for their showy and fragrant blooms. The family Amaryllidaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that includes several genera, including Galtonia and Crinum. The classification of Zephyranthes elwesii reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Amaryllidaceae family.

The plant is believed to have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever and respiratory issues. Its extracts have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and hepatoprotective activities, making it a potential candidate for the treatment of liver diseases. Additionally, Zephyranthes elwesii has been used to treat wounds and skin infections due to its antimicrobial properties. intro: Zephyranthes elwesii, also known as the Pink Rain Lily or Elwes' Zephyranth, is a species of flowering plant in the Amaryllidaceae family, native to the tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and skin problems, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Zephyranthes elwesii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Zephyranthes elwesii is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to promote immune function and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
